items.title: Bias
items.description: High bias drives underfitting — systematic error because the model class is too restrictive. This topic owns practical underfitting diagnosis; Bias owns the E[ŷ] − f*(x) definition and bias² term.

items.title: Variance
items.description: High variance drives overfitting — predictions swing with the training sample and validation error exceeds training error. Variance owns Var(ŷ) in the decomposition; this topic owns the train–val gap symptoms.

items.title: Model complexity
items.description: Increasing complexity moves from underfitting toward overfitting along the bias–variance curve. Validation curves over depth, degree, or width operationalize that movement.

items.title: Generalization
items.description: Both underfitting and overfitting are generalization failures on held-out data — too little signal learned vs too much noise memorized. Generalization owns the unseen-data performance goal and i.i.d. assumption.

items.title: Early stopping
items.description: Stopping training when validation error stops improving prevents late-epoch overfitting — especially in iterative learners. Early stopping owns the stopping rule; this topic frames when overfitting appears during training.
